title: Intensely Caramelized Dutch Goose Hash
description: A traditional Dutch dish featuring caramelized potatoes and onions in goose fat, perfect for a low-sodium breakfast or brunch. This recipe is bitter, fluffy, and adventurous.
instructions: Heat the melted goose fat in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are dark brown and caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the diced potatoes to the skillet and cook until they are tender and golden brown, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Season the mixture with low-sodium salt, black pepper, and fresh thyme.
Make two wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Cover the skillet and cook until the eggs are cooked to your desired doneness.
Serve the hash hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
